title: Centaurea epirotica
intro: Centaurea epirotica, also known as Epirotic Knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its tall stem and vibrant purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Bioactive constituents of Centaurea epirotica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Centaurea epirotica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat digestive disorders and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Centaurea epirotica is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Greece and is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation. The plant is classified as Centaurea epirotica in the genus Centaurea and in the subfamily Carduoideae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its inflorescence and leaf shape. The species is further classified as Critically Endangered by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN).
